growth_records.medium: Adapted bicarbonate-buffered CP medium (batch: 25 g/l glucose, 5 g/l yeast extract)

up.units: not stated as a specific rate; 25 g/l provided, not fully consumed

mfa.note: text; PEPCK CO2 fixation for succinate at 20% CO2

growth_records.cite: Koendjbiharie et al. Effects of CO2 limitation on the metabolism of Pseudoclostridium thermosuccinogenes. BMC Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.1186/s12866-020-01835-2. [PMC7282089]
growth_records.method: reported (growth rate shown only graphically in Fig 2c/4a; ~2-fold higher at 20% than 1% CO2; no numeric value in text)
growth_records.snippet: formate yield, which dropped from 0.53 to 0.17 mM per mM glucose during CO2 limitation... ethanol yield increased from 0.05 to 0.23 mM. Lactate yield also increased slightly (0.29 to 0.34)... succinate and acetate yields decreased slightly
growth_records.notes: 20% CO2 (CO2-excess) batch condition, RNA-seq series (Fig 3/4). Yields are mol product per mol glucose (paper writes 'mM/mM'). Growth rate only in Fig 2c/4a (no numeric value; reported >2-fold higher than at 1% CO2). ~20% of glucose C unaccounted (biomass). mfa_fluxes are flux-balance-CALCULATED cofactor generation per mol glucose from Table 1 (not measured), based on measured product yields + pathway stoichiometry from PEP; NADH/ATP of glycolysis excluded per Table 1 caption.

growth_records.cite: Koendjbiharie et al. Effects of CO2 limitation on the metabolism of Pseudoclostridium thermosuccinogenes. BMC Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.1186/s12866-020-01835-2. [PMC7282089]
growth_records.snippet: Serum bottle experiments... did show an increase in H2 production from 0.22 mol H2 per mole glucose at 20 mM NaHCO3 to 0.44 mol H2 at 1 mM NaHCO3
growth_records.notes: Serum-bottle (Fig 2a-type) CO2-replete condition (20 mM NaHCO3). Only H2 yield is numeric in text (other product yields shown graphically in Fig 2a but not numerically stated). pH ~7.4 at 60C per methods. Reported as 'data not shown' text value.

growth_records.cite: Koendjbiharie et al. Effects of CO2 limitation on the metabolism of Pseudoclostridium thermosuccinogenes. BMC Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.1186/s12866-020-01835-2. [PMC7282089]
growth_records.snippet: an increase in H2 production from 0.22 mol H2 per mole glucose at 20 mM NaHCO3 to 0.44 mol H2 at 1 mM NaHCO3, suggesting indeed that more H2 is formed during CO2 limitation
growth_records.notes: Serum-bottle CO2-limited condition (1 mM NaHCO3). Only H2 yield numeric in text (0.44 mol/mol glucose); paired with the 20 mM condition. Under CO2 limitation ethanol rises and formate falls (Fig 2a, non-numeric in text).
